requirements.evidence: ios/LaneShadow/Views/Molecules/LSTagPill.swift now applies the glass fill and border on the outer LSPill chain, and the updated LSTagPillTests source-structure assertions require the content closure to remain surface-free.

ios/LaneShadow/Views/Molecules/LSFilterChip.swift now applies selected/unselected surface styling on the outer LSPill chain, and LSFilterChipTests asserts the closure contains no background/overlay or inner horizontal padding.
The button action path remains a single LSFilterChip.dispatch call, and the focused xcodebuild test run for LaneShadowTests/LSFilterChipTests passed.

ios/LaneShadow/Views/Molecules/LSSuggestionChip.swift now keeps content-only composition inside LSPill and moves card surface styling to the outer pill chain. LSSuggestionChipTests asserts the same structural invariant.

ios/LaneShadow/Views/Molecules/LSWeatherBadge.swift now applies weather tint/border on the outer LSPill chain while preserving LSIcon/LSText content composition, and LSWeatherBadgeTests now checks the surface-modifier placement.
LSWeatherBadge still uses LSPill(size: size), and the targeted xcodebuild test run for LaneShadowTests/LSWeatherBadgeTests passed after the remediation.
The forbidden primitive grep gate remained clean across the four molecule files during the remediation validation run.
Story registration remained intact, and the targeted xcodebuild suite for the four molecule test classes passed with the strengthened source-structure assertions.

summary: APPROVED: iteration 002 fixes the core surface-placement defect by moving all pill-family surface styling onto the outer LSPill view, preserves LSIcon/LSText composition, strengthens the source-structure regression tests, and passes the requested SwiftFormat, build, test, and grep validation gates.
